svg.text: Nepal tech infrastructure — full stack reality check
What exists today vs. what is needed

g.text: TODAY: 3,878 MW hydro, seasonal surplus. Domestic grid: unstable voltage, frequent outages.
NEEDED: Dedicated industrial microgrid (±1% voltage stability). Estimated cost: NPR 80–120B.
TODAY: ~95% transit via India (BSNL/Airtel). 1 China route (Tibet). No submarine cable. ~200 Gbps total.
NEEDED: Independent IXP, 2nd China route, ideally Bangladesh cable access. ~NPR 15–25B investment.
TODAY: 3 small Tier-1/2 DCs in KTM (NTC, WorldLink, private). No Tier-3+. No GPU clusters.
NEEDED: 1 govt anchor Tier-3 DC (20–50 MW), colocation rules, disaster recovery sites outside KTM.
Layer 4 — National digital identity & data registry
TODAY: National ID rollout ~60% complete. No digital land registry. No interoperable govt DB.
NEEDED: NID API layer, digital land title system, interop standards (this is THE missing foundation).
TODAY: Fragmented portals (OCR, Inland Revenue, customs) — not interoperable. Paper forms dominate.
NEEDED: Unified API gateway, single-sign-on for citizens & businesses, open data standards.
TODAY: ConnectIPS (local), eSewa/Khalti (mobile), no Stripe/PayPal. NRB blocks FX for SaaS payments.
NEEDED: NRB sandbox for fintech, FX liberalization for digital services, NEPSE clearing house.

svg.text: Layers 1–3 = hardware. Layers 4–6 = software. Each layer blocks the one above it.
RSP's manifesto addresses layers 1, 3, 5, 6 only — layers 2 and 4 are the critical gaps.
